name: Fried Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 8 Balls Ice Cream; Any Flavor, about 1 quart
recipeInstructions:
The day before serving, make the batter. In a mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, salt, oil and egg yolks. Gradually stir in the beer. Refrigerate overnight. Also, the day before serving, coat the ice cream balls thoroughly with the crushed cornflakes. Place the ice cream balls in the coldest part of the freezer and freeze overnight.
When ready to serve, heat the peanut oil in a deep fat fryer or deep pot to 375 Degrees F (190 Degrees C). Beat the egg whites until stiff; gently fold into the batter. Coat 2 or 3 of the ice cream balls completely with the batter and add to the hot oil. Maintain the temperature at 375 Degrees F (190 Degrees C) by adjusting the heat as needed.
Fry the ice cream until golden brown, turning occasionally. Using a slotted spoon, remove the ice cream from the oil and drain briefly on paper towels. Serve immediately. Repeat with the remaining ice cream balls.
